english: I. what is uppermost 1. roof, tog °bubs-pa (thog 'bubs pa) to cover with a roof, to roof (a house) frq.; tóg gél-ba (thog gel ba) id.; also fig. to complete, to crown a thing Mil.; *tóg-sa nán-će* (thog sa nan ce) W. to roof, to finish a roof by beating and stamping down the earth or sods, of which the covering consists; tog-rdzís ytón-ba (thog rdzis gton ba) Mil. id.; also fig. to impress, c. genit., Mil. - *tóg-kár* (thog dkar) W., the opening for the smoke in a roof. - tog-ċan (thog can) having a roof, *tóg-yog* (thog 'og) W. under cover. - 2. ceiling; yá-tog (ya thog) ceiling, má-tog (ma thog) floor of a room. - 3. story, dgu-tóg (dgu thog) having nine stories or floors, frq. - 4. in a general sense: tog °drén-pa (thog 'dren pa) Mil. to be at the head, to lead, direct, govern; tog-kár (thog dkar), W. *ka-tóg-la* (kha thog la), on, upon, kyág-tog-kár (khyag thog dkar) on the ice Glr.; tóg-tu (thog tu), and tog-tóg (thog thog) adv. up, up to; above; yáṅ-tog-tu (yang thog tu) in the uppermost place, quite at the top, Glr.; postp. c. genit. (or accus.) 1. on, upon, e.g. to lay on, to place upon Pth.; sems tóg-tu lji-bar byuṅ-nas (sems thog tu lji bar byung nas) lying heavy, weighing heavily, upon one's mind Glr.; ṅai tóg-tu byuṅ (ngai thog tu byung) my heart was smitten (by that); that has touched, has grieved my heart Mil.; tog-tu kél-ba (thog tu kel ba) Mil., vb. act. to it. 2. above Glr. 3. towards, in the direction of, e.g. running towards, mai tog-tu (mai thog tu) Dzl.; yá-tog (ya thog), má-tog (ma thog) ad. above, below, or up to, up stairs, and down, down stairs Mil. 4. to, e.g. to send to Dzl. 5. dmag-tog (dmag thog) at the head of the army, or only with the army. 6. during, as long as, throughout; whilst (tog (thog) gen. without -tu (-tu)), dgun-tog (dgun thog) throughout the whole winter; *dir á-ku sem tsér tog* ('dir a khu sems tsher thog) whilst her husband is here in great anxiety Ld.; bgros-tog (bgros thog) during the walk. Cf. also ṅa-og (nga 'og), ṗi-tog (phyi thog) as sbst.: morning, evening, forenoon, afternoon W. 7. directly after, bżos-tog ḍó-ma (bzhos thog dro ma) fresh milk, S.g. (s.l.c.). - tog-nas (thog nas) 1. above, more than, *lo ṅab-ċu tog-ne ma lus* (lo ngab cu thog ne ma lus) Ld. they remained, i.e. lived not more than fifty years. 2. on the part of, Thgy., analogous to ṗyogs-nas (phyogs nas). II. thunderbolt, lightning; tog daṅ sér-ba (thog dang ser ba) lightning and hail, tog-sér-gyi ynód-pa (thog ser gyi gnod pa) damage done by the elements; tog °báb-pa (thog 'bab pa) lightning descending, rgyab-pa (rgyab pa) striking, tog-bábs-su °byón-pa (thog babs su 'byon pa) to arrive, to approach quick or suddenly like lightning Tar., resp.; tóg-gis ysód-pa (thog gis gsod pa) S.g., tog báb-ste °ċi-ba (thog bab ste 'chi ba) Do. to be killed by lightning. III. 1. fruit, produce, dkár-tog (dkar thog) v. dkár-po (dkar po); żiṅ-tog (zhing thog) produce of the fields Dzl.; lo-tóg (lo thog) a year's produce; śiṅ-tóg (shing thog) produce of a tree or other plant, fruit; ysar-tóg (gsar thog) this year's crop S.g.; tog-ṗúd (thog phud) first-fruits, as an offering; tog-żás (thog zhas) id. (?). - 2. W. fortune, wealth, property, *ṅúl-li tog* (ngul li thog) property in money, cash in hand; (s)pí-tog ((s)pi thog) common property, property belonging to a community. IV. in ma-tog (ma thog) (-tse (-tse)) for ma tag (ma thag), col. and Thgy., s.l.c., v. tag (thag). Cf. also tog-tág (thog thag), tóg-ma (thog ma), tógs-pa (thogs pa).

prob. augmentative of tog (thog), v. tóg-tu (thog tu) 6, also tag-tóg (thag thog), during, as long as, throughout; quite, mtsan tog-tág-tu (mtshan thog thag tu) all night long; nyi-ma-gċig-gi bár-du tog-tág (nyi ma gcig gi bar du thog thag) during a whole day; lam tog-tág gaṅ-ṅo (lam thog thag gang ngo) the roads were quite full (of snow) Dzl.

what is uppermost, 1. the upper end, the uppermost place, grál-gyi tóg-ma-la dúg-go (gral gyi thog ma la dug go) they sat down in the first, or uppermost, place Dzl.; gen. 2. origin, beginning; tóg-mai saṅs-rgyás kun-tu-bzán-po (thog mai sangs rgyas kun tu bzang po) Adibuddha Samantabhadra, so a deity is called, by which a prayer has been appointed that is supposed to be particularly efficacious; tóg-ma ċo-rígs mtó-ba (thog ma cho rigs mtho ba) of noble birth, as regards his origin Dzl.; tóg-ma btsás-pai tsé-na (thog ma btsas pai tshe na), tóg-ma btsás-nas (thog ma btsas nas), tóg-ma skyés-nas (thog ma skyes nas) already at his birth, from his very birth Dzl.; tóg-ma méd-pa-nas (thog ma med pa nas), dus tóg-méd-nas (dus thog med nas) time out of mind, from eternity; tóg-ma-nas (thog ma nas) from the very beginning; of itself; as a matter of course Dzl.; bsúbs-pai tog-tág-la (bsubs pai thog thag la) as soon as they began to fill up Glr.; tog-mta-bar-du (thog mtha' bar du) at first, later, in conclusion (lit. in the beginning, end, and middle) Lt.; most frq. tóg-mar (thog mar) 1. at first, first, the Lat. primum, <
